Click here for a PDF of the larger size PRO MX Fiber Reactive Dyes
Click here for a PDF of all of our PRO MX Fiber Reactive Dyes used for Ice Dyeing
CLICK THIS LINK FOR THE LIST OF MANUFACTURED MX FIBER REACTIVE DYES

Suggested Auxiliaries when using PRO MX Reactive Dyes:
PRO Print Paste Mix F or SH, PRO Dye Activator or Soda Ash, Synthrapol SP, Synthrapol LF or PROsapol, PRO Print Paste Mix F or SH, Thick F or SH, Common Salt, Metaphos and Urea

Please Note: These colors are a representation of the actual dye. Colors may vary due to your computer settings. Please test colors before beginning a project
requiring an exact color match.

PRO MX Fiber Reactive Dyes (Procion) are the most popular and versatile of all our dyes. These dyes are ideal for Tie dyeing, Ice Dyeing, Painting (Direct application), Solid Shade (Immersion Dyeing) and so much more. These dyes are designed to permanently dye cellulose fibers — plant based fibers like cotton, linen, viscose rayon, bamboo, tencel, paper, wood, basket reed and even silk at room temperature, 75° – 95° F (24°C to 35°C). They also can dye protein fibers like wool, using acid at a boil or with steam. These are the most reactive of all types of dyes and most versatile with excellent wash and light fastness properties. Available in over 135 colors ranging from delicate pastels to vibrant tones, and are completely intermixable.

CLICK THIS LINK FOR THE LIST OF PURE, MANUFACTURED MX FIBER REACTIVE DYES.

*When using these dyes for ICE DYEING, Please keep in mind that you will see the components of the mix not a solid color. For Example: When using BLACKS you will see the individual colors from the mixture and not a solid black.

Suggested Auxiliaries when using PRO MX Reactive Dyes:
PRO Print Paste Mix F or SH, PRO Dye Activator or Soda Ash, Synthrapol SP, Synthrapol LF or PROsapol, PRO Print Paste Mix F or SH, Thick F or SH, Common Salt, Metaphos and Urea

Two ounces (58 gm) will dye approximately 3 pounds or 14 yards (13 meters) of cotton muslin to a medium shade in a solid shade dye bath, or 12 T-Shirts for tie dye and can be kept in solution (without fixative) for four to five days at room temperature.

To duplicate the depth of shades shown on the MX color chips, please use this information: %OWG = amount of dye needed based upon the weight of goods

0.5%OWG = approximately ¾ level teaspoon per pound of goods
1.0%OWG = approximately 1 ½ level teaspoons per pound of goods
2.0%OWG = approximately 3 level teaspoons per pound of goods
3.0%OWG = approximately 4 ½ level teaspoons per pound of goods
4.0%OWG = approximately 6 level teaspoons per pound of goods
6.0%OWG = approximately 9 level teaspoons per pound of goods
8%OWG = approximately 12 level teaspoons per pound of goods
10.0%OWG = approximately 15 level teaspoons per pound of goods

Our PRO MX Fiber Reactive (Procion) blacks all have a slightly different cast when doing a standard immersion dye bath (not low water immersion or tie-dye).
These blacks are purchased or mixed to be dyed as black in an immersion dye bath. They are not tested as grey and could vary from lot to lot when used to dye a grey.

PRO Black 602A has a greenish cast
PRO Black 604 has a bluish cast
PRO Black 628 has a reddish cast
PRO Black 629 has a bluish cast
PRO Tie Dye Black 699 (best for Tie Dyeing) when dyed at 110F. *

PRODUCT DIRECTIONS

We are proud to offer our directions online. Explore unlimited creative possibilities and technical challenges by downloading a .pdf version of the product that you’re interested in. We recommend that before using any of our products, you review Studio Safety Guidelines to advise safety precautions and proper use of safety equipment, clean work habits and responsible use of these products.

Our directions offer English metric measuring options and temperature guidelines. You will have better control over the results if you choose the metric system, however;
it is most important that you use the measuring system that is most comfortable for you.

CLICK HERE FOR THE LIST OF PRODUCT DIRECTIONS AVAILABLE.

PRODUCT SAFETY DATA SHEETS (SDS)

SDS are an important component of product stewardship and occupational safety and health. They are intended to provide workers and emergency personnel with procedures for handling or working with products in a safe manner. SDS contain information pertaining to the particular product such as physical data, toxicity, health effects, first aid, reactivity, storage, disposal, protective equipment, and spill handling procedures

CLICK HERE FOR THE LIST OF SDS AVAILABLE.

STUDIO SAFETY GUIDELINES

In the event that you have an emergency situation after business hours 8AM-4PM EST, Monday through Friday, here are the numbers to call:

CLICK HERE TO VIEW THE STUDIO SAFETY GUIDELINES.

Emergency Telephone Numbers

800-255-3924 ChemTel. (United States)

+ 1 01 813-248-0585 (Outside the United States)

Add to Cart (Selected)

Customer review

  1. Rated 5 out of 5

    S************s – November 1, 2024

    Winter white is meant to be a liquid dye but it can be used for ice dyeing as well. As a single color ice dye it’s an off white color. No splits. If combined with other dyes I predict it would blend and create interesting results. I wish I could share a picture. If you go over to YouTube,
    Belladonna Dyes, you can see what Winter White looks like on a shirt.

  2. Rated 5 out of 5

    S************s – November 1, 2024

    Loden is a gorgeous color for ice dyeing. I definitely recommend it. I wish I could share a picture. If you go to my YouTube channel,
    Belladonna Dyes, you can see what a single color ice dye looks like. It splits very well. You won’t be disappointed.

  3. Rated 5 out of 5

    C***************i – October 18, 2024

    I love this color. Can’t find it anywhere else but here. Very light but rich.
